mission.title: clusterpedia: Use Collection Resource to list all resources in a namespace
mission.description: Use Collection Resource to list all resources in a namespace. Community-requested feature.

steps.title: Check current clusterpedia deployment
steps.description:
Verify your clusterpedia version and configuration:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n clusterpedia -l app.kubernetes.io/name=clusterpedia
helm list -n clusterpedia 2>/dev/null || echo "Not installed via Helm"
```
This feature requires a working clusterpedia installation.

steps.title: Review clusterpedia configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant clusterpedia configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n clusterpedia -l app.kubernetes.io/name=clusterpedia
kubectl get configmap -n clusterpedia -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=clusterpedia
```
### What would you like to be added?

Use Collection Resource to list all resources in a namespace

### Why is this needed?

Use Collection Resource to list all resources in a namespace

steps.title: Apply the fix for Use Collection Resource to list all resources in a namespace
steps.description:
Apply the configuration change to resolve the issue:
```yaml
$ kubectl get collectionresources kuberesources

$ kubectl get --raw "/apis/clusterpedia.io/v1beta1/collectionresources/kuberesources?onlyMetadata=true"
```

steps.title: Verify the feature works
steps.description:
Test that the new capability is working as expected:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n clusterpedia -l app.kubernetes.io/name=clusterpedia
kubectl get events -n clusterpedia --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm the feature described in "Use Collection Resource to list all resources in a namespace" is functioning correctly.

resolution.summary: See the fix PR for the community-verified solution: https://github.com/clusterpedia-io/clusterpedia/pull/186
codeSnippets:
$ kubectl get collectionresources kuberesources

$ kubectl get --raw "/apis/clusterpedia.io/v1beta1/collectionresources/kuberesources?onlyMetadata=true"

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with clusterpedia installed or the issue environment reproducible.
